snippets.label: Inspect current scene
snippets.description: Get a quick overview of objects, selection, units, and camera.
snippets.prompt: Call ping to show me the current Blender scene state — units, render engine, active camera, object counts, and what's selected. Then summarise in one paragraph.

snippets.label: List all objects
snippets.description: Enumerate every object in the scene with its type and parent.
snippets.prompt: List every object in the Blender scene. For each: name, type, and parent collection. Group the output by collection.

snippets.label: Build a simple wooden chair
snippets.description: Compose a stylised chair from primitives, properly placed.
snippets.prompt: Build a simple stylised wooden chair: seat, backrest, four legs. Use the spatial helpers (place_above, array_around) so the legs sit correctly on the floor and the seat is centred above them. Apply a wood-coloured PBR material. Take a viewport screenshot when done.

snippets.label: Create a stone arch
snippets.description: Build an arch from blocks using array helpers.
snippets.prompt: Create a stone arch made of blocks using array_around for the curved top and a stack of blocks for each pillar. Apply a rough grey stone PBR material. Centre it at the world origin and take a viewport screenshot.

snippets.label: Apply a brushed-metal PBR material
snippets.description: Assign a brushed-metal shader to the active object.
snippets.prompt: Assign a brushed-metal PBR material to the active object: dark grey base color, metallic 1.0, roughness 0.35, slight anisotropy if available. Take a viewport screenshot afterwards.

snippets.label: Build a glass shader
snippets.description: Create a clear glass material with a small amount of roughness.
snippets.prompt: Build a clear glass material on the active object using a Principled BSDF: transmission 1.0, roughness 0.05, IOR 1.45. Switch the render engine to Cycles for a real preview, then take a viewport screenshot.

snippets.label: Set up a 3-point lighting rig
snippets.description: Add key, fill, and rim lights aimed at the active object.
snippets.prompt: Set up a 3-point lighting rig aimed at the active object: a strong key light at front-right, a softer fill light at front-left, and a rim/back light behind it. Use look_at so each light points at the active object. Take a viewport screenshot.

snippets.label: Add an HDRI world
snippets.description: Build a world shader with an Environment Texture.
snippets.prompt: Build a world shader using an Environment Texture node feeding a Background. If no HDRI is available, use a Sky Texture (Nishita) with sun elevation around 30°. Take a viewport screenshot.

snippets.label: Render the active camera at 1080p
snippets.description: Set 1920x1080, render, and report the output path.
snippets.prompt: Set the render resolution to 1920x1080, render the active camera, save the result, and report the output file path. Use the existing render engine — don't switch.

snippets.label: Take a viewport screenshot
snippets.description: Quick viewport capture for visual confirmation.
snippets.prompt: Take a viewport screenshot at the current view and show it to me.
